mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rate hike months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market cooling that reduces recession probability, triggering immediate risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST yields positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized during high inflation periods demonstrates supply-side resilience, supporting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% during CPIAUCSL > 5% yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 6%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a hard-landing risk premium, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in aggressive monetary tightening.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium changes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent annualized triggers immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 1.0%, or the threshold effect disappears when using 3-quarter moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 60-day returns occur in <45% of months with payroll growth exceeding 100k.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains above 3 percent while the federal funds rate stays below 5 percent, markets anticipate delayed tightening, producing positive equity returns as growth expectations dominate.
failure_condition: The specified regime yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.
